disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe whole-body collapse and cognitive slowing came on the day after a minor physical outing, in someone who describes recurrent post-exertional crashes
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing just before this started?
Has anything like this happened after over-doing things before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I went out for a small outing yesterday and today I've completely crashed. This happens when I over-do it — I have a post-viral thing — but this time is much worse.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profoundly unrefreshing sleep — 14 hours with zero recovery
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I slept about 14 hours and woke up feeling no better at all — like I hadn't slept.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cognitive impairment — brain fog, word-finding difficulty and slowed processing
trigger_question_examples: Are you having trouble with words or concentrating?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My brain feels completely offline — I can't find words and everything is really slow and foggy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The weakness and slowing are global and symmetrical, not one-sided — no facial droop, no single-limb weakness, no slurred speech beyond exhaustion
trigger_question_examples: Is one side of your body or face weaker than the other?
Can your partner check if one side of your face is drooping, or if one arm drifts down when you hold both up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's both sides the same — my whole body is weak, not one side. My partner says my face looks even and both arms are the same.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, shivering or chills; normal skin temperature to the partner's touch
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel hot or feverish?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't feel hot and I haven't had any chills or shivering.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Marked light and sound sensitivity — wearing sunglasses in a blacked-out, silent room
trigger_question_examples: How are you coping with light and noise?
Is light or sound bothering you at the moment?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can't stand any light or noise — I've got sunglasses on and the room is completely dark and quiet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Strictly bedbound and fully dependent on the partner for hydration, feeding and repositioning; taking only sips of fluid
trigger_question_examples: What can you manage on your own right now?
Is someone there to help you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can't do anything on my own — my partner is giving me sips of fluid through a straw and turning me. I can't get up.
